resolvedQuestions.text: Aside exposes a standalone macOS CLI with direct task, exec, account, mcp, and repl modes; exact unpublished help flags remain unverified.
aside mcp is a local stdio child backed by the Aside daemon/browser and device account; no remote endpoint or MCP token is documented.

Confirmed automation is Playwright-compatible Asidewright/REPL with accessibility, interaction, screenshots, downloads, network, tabs, and visual fallback; exact MCP schemas and console support require runtime discovery.

Safe production use needs independent policy enforcement, account/daemon/profile preflight, per-profile serialization, output redaction, prompt-injection handling, and human stops for authentication and sensitive actions.

Use a five-layer CLI-primary architecture: direct task and deterministic REPL lanes, runtime-discovered Code Mode MCP fallback, one local stdio UTCP manual, caller-owned policy/evidence, and serialized mutation per profile.

keyFindings.title: Standalone CLI confirmed
keyFindings.summary: Aside ships a real task CLI distinct from its MCP and REPL modes.

keyFindings.title: Installer is macOS-only
keyFindings.summary: The official installer supports Darwin arm64/x64 and links an app-bundle executable into ~/.local/bin.

keyFindings.title: Low-level automation belongs to REPL or MCP
keyFindings.summary: Public CLI docs expose agent tasks; deterministic page inspection and screenshots are assigned to the REPL.

keyFindings.title: Exported MCP transport is local stdio
keyFindings.summary: Client config launches aside mcp as command/args; no URL or token is documented.

keyFindings.title: MCP bridge has a daemon dependency
keyFindings.summary: Idle keepalive and explicit daemon-outage fixes show the stdio child fronts a separate local service.

keyFindings.title: MCP account/auth override is undocumented
keyFindings.summary: MCP configuration has no credential and account flags are documented only for direct and exec modes.

keyFindings.title: Asidewright is the deterministic automation seam
keyFindings.summary: The REPL exposes Playwright-compatible browser semantics over a lightweight CDP-backed wrapper.

keyFindings.title: Automation capability classes are broader than published MCP evidence
keyFindings.summary: Navigation, accessibility, interaction, screenshots, downloads, tabs, network, and visual fallback are supported; exact MCP names are not published.

keyFindings.title: MCP discovery is a hard preflight
keyFindings.summary: Run initialize and tools/list and cache schemas before invoking any Aside MCP tool.

keyFindings.title: MCP needs caller-owned safety policy
keyFindings.summary: Aside's UI permission model is granular, but MCP inheritance is undocumented; enforce read/action/sensitive classes externally.

keyFindings.title: Unattended automation has human gates
keyFindings.summary: MFA, CAPTCHA, vault unlock, identity checks, and sensitive-action approvals require user presence.

keyFindings.title: Local-first has documented egress paths
keyFindings.summary: Hosted models can receive selected prompts, tool results, snapshots, screenshots, and files; sync and analytics add further processors.

keyFindings.title: Serialize mutating sessions per profile
keyFindings.summary: Profile-bound state and overlap avoidance support a conservative single-writer-per-profile rule until tested.

keyFindings.title: Reuse routing discipline, not bdg vocabulary
keyFindings.summary: Aside's CLI-first mode needs separate task and deterministic REPL lanes; typed bdg commands have no Aside evidence.

keyFindings.title: Aside UTCP registration is settled
keyFindings.summary: Register one manual named aside, type mcp, local stdio command aside with args mcp and no transport credentials.

keyFindings.title: Runtime discovery gates MCP calls
keyFindings.summary: Search/list tools, inspect callable syntax, normalize capabilities, and never guess a tool name.

keyFindings.title: Artifact proof follows Chrome standards
keyFindings.summary: Validate saved screenshots and structured captures independently instead of trusting successful tool returns.

keyFindings.title: One manual until isolation is proven
keyFindings.summary: Chrome's explicit isolated instances cannot justify parallel Aside processes; serialize per profile and probe before expansion.

ruledOutDirections.reason: No primary evidence; public docs route deterministic browser steps to REPL or MCP.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Configure exported Aside MCP as remote HTTP/SSE
ruledOutDirections.reason: Primary setup and changelog evidence identify a local stdio child.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Treat Aside MCP-client connectors as export transport
ruledOutDirections.reason: Authenticated HTTP connectors are an inbound/client feature.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Hard-code tool names from Chrome DevTools or Playwright MCP
ruledOutDirections.reason: Aside has no published tool list and exposes a code-oriented automation layer.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Require raw CDP from the model
ruledOutDirections.reason: Asidewright deliberately abstracts CDP behind Playwright-compatible semantics.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Treat local stdio as trusted by default
ruledOutDirections.reason: The process exposes logged-in browser state and powerful actions.
Aside documents human approval and authentication gates.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Allow concurrent mutating clients per profile
ruledOutDirections.reason: No isolation guarantee is published and product routines skip overlap.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Clone Chrome command names or isolated-instance layout
ruledOutDirections.reason: Aside documents task/REPL/MCP lanes and no process-isolation flag.
The repository requires progressive discovery and Aside publishes no tool list.
